title: Lydgate's Decline
description: An ambitious young doctor trapped by desire, pride, and circumstance. Watch his dreams crumble through a cascade of errors, saved only partially by Dorothea's friendship.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate pursue his ambitious medical reforms in provincial Middlemarch?
decisions.context: Lydgate arrives with grand ambitions: advance medical science, reform hospitals, make discoveries. He's brilliant but an outsider. The town is conservative. His past involves a tragic romance with an actress. ERROR #1: Too aggressive with reforms.

yes.consequence: He pushes his reforms confidently, making enemies among local medical men. His ambition blinds him to political realities.

no.consequence: AI generates path where early compromise might preserve his position.

decisions.decision: When Rosamond cries and manipulates his sympathy, will Lydgate comfort her?
decisions.context: Lydgate has NO intention of proposing. Rosamond orchestrates a moment alone, begins crying. His chivalry and desire are aroused. ERROR #2: His actions betray his judgment. Eliot's gorgeous rendering of self-entrapment.

yes.consequence: He comforts her and finds himself suddenly, impulsively engaged. He leaves an engaged man, having betrayed his own intentions. The trap closes.

no.consequence: AI generates path where he escapes the engagement trap.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate proceed with marrying Rosamond despite growing awareness of her expensive tastes?
decisions.context: Engaged now, Lydgate realizes Rosamond expects a wealthy lifestyle. He has medical debts and research ambitions. But honor binds him. ERROR #3: Proceeding despite warning signs.

yes.consequence: Honor demands it. He marries her, hoping to manage somehow. Debt begins.

no.text: No - postpone or break the engagement
no.consequence: AI generates path where he breaks free before marriage.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate accept deeper patronage from the morally questionable Bulstrode?
decisions.context: Other doctors resent Lydgate's reforms. Bulstrode offers strong support for the hospital. But there are whispers about Bulstrode's past. ERROR #4: Accepting tainted patronage.

yes.consequence: His hospital work depends on Bulstrode. He's now tied to a questionable benefactor.

no.text: No - refuse and seek other support
no.consequence: AI generates path where he maintains independence.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate continue buying expensive furnishings to please Rosamond despite his debts?
decisions.context: Rosamond has expensive tastes. Lydgate is going into debt but doesn't want to disappoint her. ERROR #5: Hiding financial reality.

yes.consequence: Debt grows. He hides the truth from Rosamond, making everything worse.

no.text: No - be honest about their finances
no.consequence: AI generates path where early honesty might save them.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate finally tell Rosamond about their desperate financial situation?
decisions.context: They're deeply in debt. Rosamond has been buying expensive furnishings, unaware. ERROR #6: He waited too long to tell her. Now it's a crisis.

yes.consequence: Rosamond is shocked and angry. She had no idea. Communication breakdown complete.

no.consequence: AI generates path where he never tells her until forced by creditors.

decisions.decision: When Rosamond suggests leaving Middlemarch to escape debts, will Lydgate refuse?
decisions.context: Rosamond wants to flee. Lydgate insists on staying to face obligations. ERROR #7: But this honor traps him further in Middlemarch.

yes.text: Yes - insist on staying and facing obligations
yes.consequence: He refuses to run away. This honor is admirable but keeps him in the trap.

no.consequence: AI generates path where they start fresh elsewhere.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate allow Rosamond to ask her wealthy uncle Sir Godwin for financial help?
decisions.context: Rosamond wants to write to her rich uncle for money. Lydgate's pride forbids it. ERROR #8: Won't let her ask for help when it might save them.

yes.text: Yes - allow her to ask her uncle
yes.consequence: AI generates path where uncle's help might save them from Bulstrode's tainted money.

no.consequence: His pride refuses charity. Rosamond does it anyway behind his back - and fails. Humiliation complete.

decisions.decision: Will Rosamond go horseback riding despite Lydgate forbidding it while pregnant?
decisions.context: Lydgate forbids riding while she's pregnant. Rosamond is miserable, feeling trapped, wants to spite him. DARK DECISION: She rides. Loses the baby. Intentional abortion? Eliot leaves it ambiguous.

yes.consequence: She rides and miscarries. Was it intentional? We don't know. The baby is lost. Their marriage darkens further.

no.text: No - obey and keep the pregnancy
no.consequence: AI generates path where the baby survives and might change their marriage.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate accept Farebrother's offer of help with his troubles?
decisions.context: Farebrother (morally good friend) perceives Lydgate's distress and offers help. Lydgate's pride refuses. ERROR #9: Rejecting true friendship. Moving away from Farebrother is always a mistake.

yes.consequence: AI generates path where friendship saves him.

no.consequence: Pride prevents him from accepting friendship. His isolation deepens. Farebrother is the best character - rejecting him is a mistake.

decisions.decision: Will Rosamond temporarily leave Lydgate to stay with her parents due to their strained marriage?
decisions.context: Their marriage is miserable. Rosamond leaves for her parents' house. Separation begins.

yes.consequence: She leaves. They're living separately now. Marriage nearly dissolved.

no.text: No - stay and work on the marriage
no.consequence: AI generates path where they try to reconcile earlier.

decisions.decision: Will Lydgate accept Bulstrode's loan of £1000?
decisions.context: Lydgate is desperate. Bulstrode offers £1000 RIGHT AFTER Raffles dies upstairs (with questionable care from Bulstrode). ERROR #10: The fatal compromise. Accepting tainted money at the worst moment.

yes.consequence: He accepts out of desperation. The timing makes it look like payment for silence about Raffles' death. His reputation will be destroyed.

no.consequence: AI generates path where he preserves integrity despite hardship.

decisions.decision: When the Bulstrode-Raffles scandal breaks, will Lydgate stay to defend himself?
decisions.context: Raffles' death and Bulstrode's crimes are exposed. Lydgate, who took the loan and attended Raffles, is suspected of complicity. Scandal and disgrace. Rosamond wants to flee immediately.

yes.text: Yes - stay and try to defend his integrity
yes.consequence: He tries to defend himself but the scandal sticks. His medical reputation in Middlemarch is ruined.

no.consequence: AI generates path where they flee before the scandal fully breaks.

decisions.decision: Will Rosamond continue encouraging Will Ladislaw's visits and flirtations?
decisions.context: Rosamond is unhappy in her marriage. Will Ladislaw pays attention to her. She encourages his visits and flirts. This is disrespectful to Lydgate and will hurt Dorothea (who witnesses it).

yes.consequence: She flirts with Will, disrespecting Lydgate. Dorothea sees this and is devastated. The flirtation poisons multiple relationships.

no.consequence: AI generates path where she focuses on her marriage instead.

decisions.decision: After Dorothea comes to defend Lydgate, will Rosamond respect her enough to stick with her marriage?
decisions.context: Dorothea's visit and high opinion of Lydgate shows Rosamond what true respect looks like. Dorothea believes in Lydgate's integrity. This might help R appreciate her husband more. Also, social status matters - D is gentlefolk.

yes.text: Yes - learn to respect Lydgate more
yes.consequence: Rosamond learns from Dorothea's respect for Lydgate. She sticks with the marriage (though never truly happy). They stay together.

no.consequence: AI generates path where the marriage dissolves completely.
